seed_problem: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.
mutation: input: skin as a deep-sea cable network dual-landing-station bisection and plant a random (index-uncorrelated) balanced community structure with heavy inter-community cables plus a lighter intra-community noise layer, so the checker's fixed index-block reference bisection is a genuinely mediocre baseline while a good balanced cut collects far more cross-shore bandwidth; objective stays balanced maximum-cut (maximize inter-station trunk weight subject to |count0-count1|<=1).
title: Deep-Sea Cable Network: Dual Landing-Station Bisection
strategies: index-block split (reproduces the checker baseline B, Ratio=0.1)
Kernighan-Lin style balance-preserving swaps run to convergence
multi-restart local search from perturbed balanced starts keeping the best cut
spectral / planted-community recovery followed by balanced rounding
